name: Charlie Barnett
blurb: He auditioned for Saturday Night Live in 1980 but did not follow through, and the cast spot went to Eddie Murphy.
bio: Charlie Barnett was an American comedian who built a legendary reputation performing raucous, improvised stand-up to crowds in New York City parks, most famously around the fountain in Washington Square Park. He auditioned for Saturday Night Live in 1980 but did not follow through, and the cast spot went to Eddie Murphy. He later played Noogie Lamont on Miami Vice and mentored younger comics including Dave Chappelle before his death from AIDS in 1996.

timeline.ev: Auditioned for SNL; spot ultimately went to Eddie Murphy

Died of AIDS-related illness at age 41
Died March 16 of AIDS at age 41

accolades: Renowned Washington Square Park street comedian and mentor to Dave Chappelle
Renowned Washington Square Park street comedian; mentor to Dave Chappelle and an influence on Jeff Ross

flags.bits_honest_gap_note: No embeddable OFFICIAL footage found. Charlie Barnett (d.1996, Washington Square Park street comic) has scattered YouTube footage but every located clip is a fan/curator re-upload, not an official channel, estate, or rights-holder: Washington Square Park sets (scipjlqqrDg, etc.) are on fan channel 'SorryHaters'; 'Def Comedy Jam 2' (Wxs6rs2_9KY) on fan channel 'The Golden Era'; 'Showtime at the Apollo' (bE-VZktPsis) on personal channel 'H Barnwell'; his 1986 Cinemax special 'Terms of Enrollment' (iimnx9CbN-c) on the curator/archive channel 'Reelblack One' (a third-party re-upload, NOT the Cinemax/rights-holder). No official Carson or Letterman archive upload of his appearances surfaced, no estate channel exists, and no 'Charlie Barnett - Topic' album-audio channel was found. Per the no-fan-rips rule, nothing is listed. Honest gap.
